file.name: rgb-to-ycbcr.js
file.description: Converts RGB to YCbCr. Ranges: R, G, B: [0, 255] Y: [0, 255] Cb, Cr: [0, 255] (centered at 128)

file.name: forward-dct.js
file.description: Discrete Cosine Transform (DCT) The DCT is the heart of JPEG compression. It converts a block of spatial data (pixels) into the frequency domain. - Low frequencies (large features, background) end up in the top-left corner. - High frequencies (fine details, noise) end up in the bottom-right corner. This allows us to later discard (quantize) the high-frequency data which the human eye is less sensitive to, achieving compression.

file.name: inverse-dct-spec.js
file.description: IDCT Implementations from JPEG T.81 Specification This file contains three IDCT implementations: 1. idctPureRef - Pure reference from spec (no optimizations) 2. idctOptimizedRef - Separable transform optimization 3. idctFastAAN - Fast AAN algorithm

file.name: inverse-dct.js
file.description: Inverse Discrete Cosine Transform (IDCT) Converts frequency domain coefficients back to spatial domain pixel values. This implementation matches the encoder's forward DCT using the same cosine table approach. JPEG spec Annex A describes the DCT/IDCT mathematics.

file.name: block-assembly.js
file.description: Block Assembly - Assemble decoded 8x8 blocks into full image This module handles: - Placing blocks in correct positions - MCU interleaving - Cropping to actual image dimensions - Converting to RGBA ImageData format

file.name: blocks.js
file.description: Pads dimensions to a multiple of 16 for 4:2:0 subsampling. In 4:2:0, MCUs are 16x16 pixels (2x2 luma blocks + 1 Cb + 1 Cr).

file.name: downsampling.js
file.description: Chroma Downsampling - Downsample chroma components for 4:2:0 encoding For 4:2:0 subsampling, chroma (Cb/Cr) is sampled at half the resolution of luma in both horizontal and vertical directions.

file.name: forward-zigzag.js
file.description: Undocumented module

file.name: inverse-zigzag.js
file.description: Inverse ZigZag - Converts 1D coefficient array to 8x8 2D block The inverse of the zigzag reordering used during encoding. JPEG spec lines 149-158 define the zigzag order.

file.name: upsampling.js
file.description: Chroma Upsampling - Upsample subsampled chroma components to match luma resolution JPEG commonly uses chroma subsampling (4:2:0, 4:2:2) to reduce file size. This module upsamples Cb and Cr components back to full resolution.

file.name: forward-quantization.js
file.description: Quantizes a block of DCT coefficients.

file.name: inverse-quantization.js
file.description: Dequantization - Multiply quantized coefficients by quantization table values This reverses the quantization step from encoding. JPEG spec lines 135-141 describe the dequantization process.

file.name: bit-core.js
file.description: Pure functional core for Bit Reading operations. Return tuple format: [value, newByteOffset, newBitOffset, error]

file.name: bit-reader-optimized.js
file.description: Stateful wrapper for BitReader operations. Delegates all logic to pure functional primitives in bit-core.js.

file.name: bit-reader.js
file.description: BitReader Wrapper Delegates to the Optimized implementation

file.name: bit-writer.js
file.description: Writes a 2-byte marker (e.g. 0xFFDA) ensuring alignment and NO byte stuffing.

file.name: huffman-decoder-fsm.js
file.description: Huffman Decoder - Decodes Huffman-encoded bitstream into quantized coefficients Implements DC differential decoding and AC run-length decoding as specified in JPEG spec lines 119-134

file.name: huffman-encoder-fsm.js
file.description: Encodes a single 8x8 block of quantized DCT coefficients.

file.name: huffman-optimized-fsm.js
file.description: Optimized Huffman decoding implementation (O(1)) Uses a 16-bit lookup table (Uint16Array)

file.name: dht-automaton.js
file.description: Huffman Table Parser - Parses DHT (Define Huffman Table) segments DHT segment format (JPEG spec lines 79-86): - Lh (16 bits): Segment length - Loop while bytes remaining: - Tc_Th (8 bits): Table class (high 4 bits: 0=DC, 1=AC) | Table ID (low 4 bits) - BITS[1..16] (16 bytes): Number of codes of each length - HUFFVAL (variable): Symbol values

file.name: dqt-automaton.js
file.description: Quantization Table Parser - Parses DQT (Define Quantization Table) segments DQT segment format (JPEG spec lines 71-77): - Lq (16 bits): Segment length - Loop while bytes remaining: - Pq_Tq (8 bits): Precision (high 4 bits) | Table ID (low 4 bits) - Elements (64 × 8 or 16 bits): Quantization values in zigzag order

file.name: marker-scanner.js
file.description: MarkerParser - Parses JPEG file structure and extracts segments JPEG files consist of markers (0xFFxx) followed by optional segment data. This module provides functions to locate markers and extract their payloads.

file.name: sof-automaton.js
file.description: Frame Header Parser - Parses SOF0 (Start of Frame, Baseline DCT) segment SOF0 segment format (JPEG spec lines 42-55): - Lf (16 bits): Frame header length - P (8 bits): Sample precision (8 for baseline) - Y (16 bits): Number of lines (height) - X (16 bits): Number of samples per line (width) - Nf (8 bits): Number of components (1=grayscale, 3=color) - For each component: - C (8 bits): Component identifier - H_V (8 bits): Sampling factors (high 4 bits: H, low 4 bits: V) - Tq (8 bits): Quantization table selector

file.name: sos-automaton.js
file.description: Scan Header Parser - Parses SOS (Start of Scan) segment SOS segment format (JPEG spec lines 57-69): - Ls (16 bits): Scan header length - Ns (8 bits): Number of components in scan - For each component: - Cs (8 bits): Component selector - Td_Ta (8 bits): DC/AC table selectors (high 4: DC, low 4: AC) - Ss (8 bits): Start of spectral selection (0 for sequential) - Se (8 bits): End of spectral selection (63 for sequential) - Ah_Al (8 bits): Successive approximation (0 for sequential)

file.name: spiff-parser.js
file.description: SPIFF Parser Parses SPIFF (Still Picture Interchange File Format) headers from APP8 markers. Based on ISO/IEC 10918-3.

file.name: decoder.js
file.description: JPEG Decoder - Main class that orchestrates the complete decoding pipeline Decodes baseline sequential DCT JPEG files with Huffman coding. Supports grayscale and color images with various chroma subsampling (4:4:4, 4:2:2, 4:2:0).

file.name: encoder.js
file.description: JpegEncoder This class implements a basic JPEG encoder.

file.name: file-scanner.js
file.description: FileScanner - Handles directory scanning using File System Access API

file.name: transcoder.js
file.description: JpegTranscoder Updates steganography data in JPEG files WITHOUT generation loss. Implementation: Uses direct DCT coefficient manipulation (lossless). This prevents quality degradation when updating the password vault repeatedly.

file.name: aes-gcm.js
file.description: Encryption - Handles AES-GCM encryption/decryption

file.name: crypto-compat.js
file.description: Undocumented module

file.name: index.js
file.description: Undocumented module

file.name: pbkdf2.js
file.description: KeyDerivation - Handles password-based key derivation Uses PBKDF2 with SHA-256

file.name: galois-field.js
file.description: Undocumented module

file.name: index.js
file.description: Undocumented module

file.name: reed-solomon-codec.js
file.description: Undocumented module

file.name: reedsolomon.js
file.description: Undocumented module

file.name: rs-interleaver.js
file.description: ECC Profiles for Reed-Solomon encoding. All profiles use RS(255, k) over GF(256). | Profile | Data (k) | Parity | Overhead | Correctable Symbols | |----------|----------|--------|----------|---------------------| | Low | 251 | 4 | 1.6% | 2 | | Medium | 223 | 32 | 14% | 16 | | High | 191 | 64 | 33% | 32 | | Ultra | 127 | 128 | 100% | 64 | | Extreme | 85 | 170 | 200% | 85 |

file.name: batch-embedder.js
file.description: BatchEmbedder - Handles embedding data across multiple images

file.name: batch-extractor.js
file.description: BatchExtractor - Handles extracting and reassembling data from multiple images

file.name: chunk-manager.js
file.description: ChunkManager - Handles splitting large data into chunks and reassembling them

file.name: f5-syndrome.js
file.description: F5 Steganography Implementation Implements the F5 algorithm for hiding data in JPEG coefficients. F5 uses matrix encoding for improved embedding efficiency and correctly handles "shrinkage" when coefficients become 0. Key features: - Matrix encoding (k bits embedded changing at most 1 coefficient per n-group) - Permutation of coefficients for uniform change distribution - Shrinkage handling via re-embedding

file.name: index.js
file.description: Undocumented module

file.name: jsteg.js
file.description: Jsteg Steganography Implementation Implements the Jsteg algorithm for hiding data in JPEG coefficients. Jsteg embeds data in the Least Significant Bit (LSB) of non-zero quantized DCT coefficients. It skips: - DC coefficients (index 0 of each block) to preserve overall luminance/color. - Coefficients with value 0 or 1 (though some variants use 1, standard Jsteg usually skips 0 and 1 to avoid ambiguity, but simple LSB replacement on non-zeros is common. Let's stick to: skip 0s, and maybe skip 1s if it causes issues, but usually just skip 0s is the definition of "non-zero". Wait, Jsteg specifically skips 0 and 1? Actually, the classic Jsteg algorithm: - Sequential embedding. - Skips DC coefficients. - Skips AC coefficients that are 0 or 1. - Replaces LSB of other coefficients. However, skipping 1s reduces capacity significantly. A simpler variant is "LSB of non-zeros". Let's implement "LSB of non-zeros" first (skipping only 0). If we encounter issues, we can refine. Actually, let's stick to skipping 0s only for max capacity for now, unless we find a reason not to. Data Format: [Length (32-bit BE)][Data Payload]

file.name: payload-container.js
file.description: Container Format for Steganographic Data Storage Format: [Magic Bytes: 4] "JSTG" - format identifier [Version: 1] Format version (currently 1) [Flags: 1] Bit flags (encrypted, compressed, chunked, etc.) [Metadata Length: 2] Length of JSON metadata in bytes [Metadata: N] JSON metadata (filename, encryption params, etc.) [Payload Length: 4] Actual data length [Payload: N] The data itself [CRC32: 4] Integrity checksum

file.name: code-quality.md
file.description: Undocumented module

file.name: constitution.md
file.description: Undocumented module

file.name: test-specs.md
file.description: Undocumented module

file.name: AAN-algo-spec.xml
file.description: Undocumented module

file.name: AAN-forward-algo-spec.xml

file.name: fastIDCT-algo-spec.xml
file.description: Undocumented module

file.name: secure-record.js
file.description: Use SecureEntry.create() instead.

file.name: crc32.js
file.description: CRC32 Checksum Utility Uses optimized lookup table implementation.

file.name: main.js
file.description: Main entry point for the JPEG Encoder/Decoder application. Initializes UI modules.
